mission.title: knative: Add option to enable/disable profiling of Knative Serving pods
mission.description: Add option to enable/disable profiling of Knative Serving pods. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the feature

Make it possible to profile Knative Serving while it's running.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add option to enable/disable profiling of Knative Serving…
steps.description:
* Introduce a new port 8008 where a web server will be listening and serving profiling data that are consumable by the pprof tool
* Introduce profiling.enable flag in config-observability.yaml that will be either true or false. It will indicate whether it's possible to access the URLs that server
```yaml
2) Use port-forwarding to get access to Knative Serving pods
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add option to enable/disable profiling of Knative Serving…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
* Introduce a new port 8008 where a web server will be listening and serving profiling data that are consumable by the pprof tool
* Introduce profiling.enable flag in config-observability.yaml that will be either true or false. It will indicate whether it's possible to access the URLs that server pprof-specific profiling data.
codeSnippets: 2) Use port-forwarding to get access to Knative Serving pods
3) Use either of these commands (detailed description at [url](https://golang.org/src/net/http/pprof/pprof.go)) to get/analyze pprof data:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
